old_claim.note: Programmable Cas9 nuclease activity is the core platform for early CRISPR genome editing.

new_claim.note: Early CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing requires Cas9 nuclease activity as the programmable DNA-cutting component directed by guide RNA.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and early experimental sections describing mammalian-cell genome engineering with S. pyogenes Cas9 and guide RNAs.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper demonstrates CRISPR/Cas genome editing by reconstituting Cas9 nuclease activity with guide RNAs in mammalian cells and measuring targeted genome modification.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source licenses a required method edge because Cas9 is not background context in the demonstrated workflow; it is the programmable DNA-cleaving enzyme paired with guide RNA to produce the edited locus.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing keeps programmable Cas9 nuclease as a required component; only evidence level, confidence, source specificity, and note improve.
would_reject_if: The cited source only described in vitro Cas9 programmability and did not demonstrate genome editing in cells.
The edge note claimed all CRISPR genome editing requires Cas9, rather than the Cas9-specific early genome-editing platform represented by this node.
The edge was weakened to enabling despite the node being explicitly scoped to CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing.
short_reason: The 2013 primary genome-engineering paper directly supports Cas9 nuclease activity as a required component of early CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing.
